basic_details.job_title: CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 27 Fitter, Electrician and More Posts
basic_details.job_location: CSIR-CMERI, Durgapur (West Bengal) and Centre of Excellence for Farm Machinery, Ludhiana (Punjab)
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Screening → Trade Test (qualifying) → Competitive Written Examination
basic_details.short_information: CSIR-Central Mechanical Engineering Research Institute (CSIR-CMERI), Durgapur, has released Advertisement No. 01/2026 (No. 8/2/2026(1)-Rct dated 4 July 2026) inviting online applications for 27 posts of Technician (1) [Group-II] across seven trades at CSIR-CMERI Durgapur (West Bengal) and its Extension Centre, the Centre of Excellence for Farm Machinery, Ludhiana (Punjab). Eligible candidates can apply online at www.cmeri.res.in from 4 July 2026 (10:00 AM IST) to 3 August 2026 (5:00 PM IST).

application_fee.details: A non-refundable fee of Rs 500 is mandatory for applicants other than Ex-Servicemen, SC, ST, Women, and PwBD candidates, who are exempted from the fee. | The fee must be deposited online through State Bank Collect (SBI Collect) only; fees paid through any other mode will not be accepted, and the application will be treated as without fee and rejected. The transaction number/receipt number generated after successful payment must be mentioned at the appropriate place in the online application before submission. Candidates must download the e-receipt from SBI Collect and upload it in the online application, and are advised to preserve it for future reference. A candidate can apply for multiple post codes subject to fulfilling eligibility criteria for each; primary/registration details need to be submitted only once, but the application form (except registration details) must be filled and the fee paid separately for each post code. Fee once paid will not be refunded on any count, and the application once finally submitted cannot be withdrawn or modified.

age_limit.details: The minimum and maximum age, as on the closing date of online application (3 August 2026), must be 18 years and 28 years respectively. The cut-off date for determining age, qualifications, and claims of SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/EWS/PwBD status or any other benefit (fee exemption, reservation, age relaxation, etc.), where not specified otherwise, is the last date for submission of online application, i.e., 3 August 2026. | Additional permissible relaxation in upper age limit for Group ‘C’ posts: | Key conditions on age relaxation: | No age relaxation is allowed to SC/ST/OBC candidates applying against unreserved posts. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates must upload a copy of the certificate in the prescribed format, duly signed by the competent authority. OBC candidates must produce the Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) certificate valid for appointment to posts under the Central Government, and must ensure they do not fall in the creamy layer as on 3 August 2026. Age relaxation for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) is given only to candidates with 40% or more disability holding a valid certificate. As per Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment O.M. No. 18-25/2024-Policy dated 17 December 2025, disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability category) are not eligible for reservation, while conditions that are "progressive, non-progressive, or not likely to improve" are eligible. Candidates with a temporary disability certificate bearing conditions other than "likely to improve" must get themselves re-assessed under the RPwD Amendment Rules 2024. Candidates claiming age relaxation as a widow, divorced woman, or woman judicially separated from her husband must produce specific documentary evidence (death certificate with affidavit of non-remarriage for widows; certified copy of the court judgment/decree along with an affidavit of non-remarriage for divorced/judicially separated women) issued by the competent authority.

qualification.details: For all seven trades, the essential qualification follows a common pattern based on SSC/10th Standard with Science subjects and minimum 55% marks, combined with an ITI/trade certificate or equivalent work experience. Desirable experience is trade-specific and is summarized below. | Essential Qualification: SSC / 10th Standard or equivalent with Science subjects, minimum 55% marks, and an ITI certificate or national/state trade certificate in Fitter from a recognized institution; OR the same qualification with 2 years’ full-time apprentice trainee experience in Fitter from a recognized institution; OR the same qualification with 3 years’ work experience in Fitter in a Ministry/Department/Organization/PSU/Autonomous Body under the Government of India/State/UT. | Desirable Experience: Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and schematics; proficiency in fitting, assembling, and maintaining mechanical parts and systems/facilities; knowledge of tools and machinery used in fittings (drills, tap, hacksaw, etc.); interpreting blueprints, technical drawings, and specifications; basic knowledge of computer systems and AutoCAD software; ability to work in a team and perform hard work in physically demanding conditions during field trials. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Electrician / Mechanic (Electrical Power Drives). | Desirable Experience: Understanding of electrical circuits, wiring, soldering, and components; knowledge of single-phase and three-phase systems/motors and power supply management; ability to install, repair, and maintain electrical systems with DC & AC (1ph & 3ph) equipment, crimping lugs, cable glands and connections; conversance with electrical tools (multi-meter, circuit/tong tester); ability to diagnose and repair faults in circuits and systems like power converters and drives; knowledge of electrical safety standards; teamwork and physically demanding fieldwork ability. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Electronics Mechanic / Instrument Mechanic. | Desirable Experience: Proficiency in repairing and maintaining electronic equipment, circuits, and systems; troubleshooting electrical/electronic faults; familiarity with tools and instruments for electronic repairs and measurements (multi-meters, circuit/tong tester); PCB making, soldering, precision wiring, cable routing, testing of components; motor and sensor wiring/testing; programming of embedded boards/micro-controllers; basic knowledge of computer systems and diagnostic software; teamwork and fieldwork ability. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Machinist. | Desirable Experience: Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, dimensions, and machining tolerances; proficiency operating lathe, milling, drilling, and grinding machines; knowledge of cutting tools, feeds, speeds, and machining parameters; fabrication of precision components, fixtures, and adaptors; understanding of measurement instruments (vernier calipers, micrometers, dial gauges); basic knowledge of CNC machining/programming and CAD drawings; teamwork and workshop/field work ability during fabrication and trials. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Foundryman. | Desirable Experience: Knowledge of foundry processes including moulding, casting, and core making; ability to interpret basic drawings/specifications for casting requirements; familiarity with melting, pouring, and solidification of metals; knowledge of moulding materials, sand preparation, and pattern handling; ability to identify casting defects and take corrective action; basic knowledge of heat treatment and finishing processes; understanding of foundry safety practices; teamwork and physically demanding workshop work ability. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Mechanic (Motor Vehicle/Diesel). | Desirable Experience: Knowledge of mechanical systems including engines, transmissions, and auxiliary systems; ability to diagnose faults and carry out repair/maintenance; familiarity with hydraulic and pneumatic systems in vehicles and machinery; ability to read service manuals, schematics, and technical documentation; knowledge of maintenance/repair tools and equipment; basic understanding of fuel, lubrication, and cooling systems; assisting in maintenance of engines, pumps, and test rigs; basic knowledge of computer-based diagnostic tools; teamwork and fieldwork ability under demanding conditions. | Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning. | Desirable Experience: Knowledge of refrigeration cycles, air-conditioning systems, and HVAC fundamentals; ability to install, operate, and maintain refrigeration/AC systems; familiarity with compressors, condensers, evaporators, and control components; ability to read system layouts, piping diagrams, and schematics; knowledge of refrigerants, charging procedures, and leak detection; understanding of electrical components in HVAC systems; ability to troubleshoot faults and carry out preventive maintenance; basic knowledge of computer systems and control interfaces; teamwork and workshop/field work ability.

selection_process: Mode of Examination OMR based or Computer Based Objective Type Multiple Choice Examination
Medium of Questions English and Hindi, except questions on the English Language paper
Standard of Examination SSC + ITI / 12th Standard
Paper Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Negative Marking
Paper-I (1 hour) Mental Ability Test (General Intelligence, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, Problem Solving, Situational Judgement) 50 100 (2 marks per correct answer) No negative marking
Paper-II (30 minutes) General Awareness 25 75 (3 marks per correct answer) 1 mark deducted per wrong answer
Paper-II (30 minutes) English Language 25 75 (3 marks per correct answer) 1 mark deducted per wrong answer
Paper-III (1 hour) Concerned Subject (based on advertised qualification for the post) 50 150 (3 marks per correct answer) 1 mark deducted per wrong answer
Selection is a three-stage process: screening of applications
and a Competitive Written Examination. This section explains the marking scheme and qualifying criteria for the written stage.
Online applications received within the stipulated last date are screened by a duly constituted Screening Committee

which shortlists candidates for a Trade Test in the relevant trade. The Trade Test is qualifying in nature; its syllabus will be notified separately on the official website. Candidates who qualify in the Trade Test are called for the Competitive Written Examination.

Paper-II and Paper-III are evaluated only for candidates who secure the minimum threshold marks (to be determined by the Selection Committee) in Paper-I. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of marks obtained in Paper-II and Paper-III only. Resolution of Tie Cases (as per CSIR Letter No. 5-1(211)/2014-PD dated 30 May 2023)

in order of preference: higher marks in the concerned subject/trade paper; lesser negative marks in the concerned subject/trade paper; lesser negative marks in the papers considered for merit; older date of birth; candidate acquiring the essential degree earlier; and alphabetical order of first names.
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply ONLINE only through the official website www.cmeri.res.in; no other mode of application will be considered. The online application window is open from 4 July 2026, 10:00 AM, to 3 August 2026, 5:00 PM.

Create/Verify Email ID – Candidates without a valid email ID should create one before applying. The registered email address and mobile number should remain active till the completion of the recruitment process, as communication will be made by CSIR-CMERI.
Register on the Portal – Register with name and email address; after successful registration, log in using the credentials provided to access the Online Application Portal.
Fill Application & Upload Documents – Complete the application form and upload all relevant supporting documents, ensuring the application is accurate and complete before finalizing.
Pay Application Fee (wherever applicable) – Pay the fee online via SBI Collect, download the e-receipt, and upload it in the application along with the payment details at the appropriate place.

Submit the Application – Click the ‘Submit’ button to finally submit the form. No further modifications or amendments will be permitted after submission. Keep a printout of the completed application form; candidates should NOT send a hard copy of the application after submission.

Candidates from Universities/Institutes awarding CGPA/SGPA/OGPA/DGPA/CPI grades must convert these into percentage as per their University/Institute formula and upload a copy of the conversion formula issued by the Board/University/Institute. Print-outs of the application and payment details will not be available after 5:00 PM on 3 August 2026.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to apply well in time without waiting for the last date for submission of online applications. Date, time, and venue of the Trade Test and Written Examination, along with any addendum/corrigendum, will be intimated only through the CSIR-CMERI website.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CMERI Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The notification covers a single post, Technician (1) [Group-II], with 27 vacancies spread across 7 trades. The table below answers "how many posts and in which trade" at a glance. Overall, the 27 posts carry the reservation break-up of SC-04, OBC(NCL)-05, UR-PwBD(HH)-01, UR-PwBD(OH)-01, EWS-04, and UR-12. Out of these 27 posts, 2 posts are reserved horizontally for Ex-Servicemen (ESM). The post carries Pay Level-2 (₹19,900 – ₹63,200) as per the 7th CPC, with total emoluments of approximately ₹39,000 per month (inclusive of Basic Pay, DA, HRA, and TA as admissible). Note: The number of vacancies indicated is provisional and may increase or decrease at the time of actual selection. Selected candidates may be posted to work at any division as per suitability and requirement, may be required to work in shifts and attend emergencies at odd hours, and may be required to assist in interrelated activities of their duties (proposal preparation, estimation, tender, work execution, billing, coordination, and liaison with other parties).

content_sections.heading: Age Limit and Relaxation
content_sections.text: The minimum and maximum age, as on the closing date of online application (3 August 2026), must be 18 years and 28 years respectively. The cut-off date for determining age, qualifications, and claims of SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/EWS/PwBD status or any other benefit (fee exemption, reservation, age relaxation, etc.), where not specified otherwise, is the last date for submission of online application, i.e., 3 August 2026. Additional permissible relaxation in upper age limit for Group ‘C’ posts: Key conditions on age relaxation: No age relaxation is allowed to SC/ST/OBC candidates applying against unreserved posts. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates must upload a copy of the certificate in the prescribed format, duly signed by the competent authority. OBC candidates must produce the Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) certificate valid for appointment to posts under the Central Government, and must ensure they do not fall in the creamy layer as on 3 August 2026. Age relaxation for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) is given only to candidates with 40% or more disability holding a valid certificate. As per Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment O.M. No. 18-25/2024-Policy dated 17 December 2025, disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability category) are not eligible for reservation, while conditions that are "progressive, non-progressive, or not likely to improve" are eligible. Candidates with a temporary disability certificate bearing conditions other than "likely to improve" must get themselves re-assessed under the RPwD Amendment Rules 2024. Candidates claiming age relaxation as a widow, divorced woman, or woman judicially separated from her husband must produce specific documentary evidence (death certificate with affidavit of non-remarriage for widows; certified copy of the court judgment/decree along with an affidavit of non-remarriage for divorced/judicially separated women) issued by the competent authority.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification & Desirable Experience (Trade-wise)
content_sections.text: For all seven trades, the essential qualification follows a common pattern based on SSC/10th Standard with Science subjects and minimum 55% marks, combined with an ITI/trade certificate or equivalent work experience. Desirable experience is trade-specific and is summarized below. Essential Qualification: SSC / 10th Standard or equivalent with Science subjects, minimum 55% marks, and an ITI certificate or national/state trade certificate in Fitter from a recognized institution; OR the same qualification with 2 years’ full-time apprentice trainee experience in Fitter from a recognized institution; OR the same qualification with 3 years’ work experience in Fitter in a Ministry/Department/Organization/PSU/Autonomous Body under the Government of India/State/UT. Desirable Experience: Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and schematics; proficiency in fitting, assembling, and maintaining mechanical parts and systems/facilities; knowledge of tools and machinery used in fittings (drills, tap, hacksaw, etc.); interpreting blueprints, technical drawings, and specifications; basic knowledge of computer systems and AutoCAD software; ability to work in a team and perform hard work in physically demanding conditions during field trials. Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Electrician / Mechanic (Electrical Power Drives). Desirable Experience: Understanding of electrical circuits, wiring, soldering, and components; knowledge of single-phase and three-phase systems/motors and power supply management; ability to install, repair, and maintain electrical systems with DC & AC (1ph & 3ph) equipment, crimping lugs, cable glands and connections; conversance with electrical tools (multi-meter, circuit/tong tester); ability to diagnose and repair faults in circuits and systems like power converters and drives; knowledge of electrical safety standards; teamwork and physically demanding fieldwork ability. Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Electronics Mechanic / Instrument Mechanic. Desirable Experience: Proficiency in repairing and maintaining electronic equipment, circuits, and systems; troubleshooting electrical/electronic faults; familiarity with tools and instruments for electronic repairs and measurements (multi-meters, circuit/tong tester); PCB making, soldering, precision wiring, cable routing, testing of components; motor and sensor wiring/testing; programming of embedded boards/micro-controllers; basic knowledge of computer systems and diagnostic software; teamwork and fieldwork ability. Essential Qualification: Same qualification pattern as above, specific to Machinist. Desirable Experience: Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, dimensions, and machining tolerances; proficiency operating lathe, milling, drilling, and grinding machines; knowledge of cutting tools, feeds, speeds, and machining parameters; fabrication of precision components, fixtures, and adaptors;

content_sections.heading: Exam Pattern & Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is a three-stage process: screening of applications, a qualifying Trade Test, and a Competitive Written Examination. This section explains the marking scheme and qualifying criteria for the written stage. Online applications received within the stipulated last date are screened by a duly constituted Screening Committee, which shortlists candidates for a Trade Test in the relevant trade. The Trade Test is qualifying in nature ; its syllabus will be notified separately on the official website. Candidates who qualify in the Trade Test are called for the Competitive Written Examination. Paper-II and Paper-III are evaluated only for candidates who secure the minimum threshold marks (to be determined by the Selection Committee) in Paper-I. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of marks obtained in Paper-II and Paper-III only. Resolution of Tie Cases (as per CSIR Letter No. 5-1(211)/2014-PD dated 30 May 2023), in order of preference: higher marks in the concerned subject/trade paper; lesser negative marks in the concerned subject/trade paper; lesser negative marks in the papers considered for merit; older date of birth; candidate acquiring the essential degree earlier; and alphabetical order of first names.

content_sections.heading: Benefits Under Council Service
content_sections.text: These posts carry usual allowances – Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA) (not admissible if staying in the Institute’s colony), and Transport Allowance (TA), as admissible to Central Government/CSIR Council employees. Employees are also eligible for accommodation of their entitled type as per CSIR Allotment Rules, subject to availability. In addition to emoluments, benefits such as reimbursement of Medical Expenses, Leave Travel Concession, and House Building Advance are available as per extant CSIR rules. Selected candidates will be governed by the National Pension System or the extant CSIR pension provisions. CSIR provides opportunities for career advancement under the Assessment Promotion Scheme for Technical & Support Staff, subject to qualifying as per rules amended from time to time. Selected candidates will be placed on probation for one year from the date of joining, extendable/curtailable at the Appointing Authority’s discretion, after which they will be considered for confirmation as per extant rules.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: A non-refundable fee of Rs 500 is mandatory for applicants other than Ex-Servicemen, SC, ST, Women, and PwBD candidates, who are exempted from the fee. The fee must be deposited online through State Bank Collect (SBI Collect) only; fees paid through any other mode will not be accepted, and the application will be treated as without fee and rejected. The transaction number/receipt number generated after successful payment must be mentioned at the appropriate place in the online application before submission. Candidates must download the e-receipt from SBI Collect and upload it in the online application, and are advised to preserve it for future reference. A candidate can apply for multiple post codes subject to fulfilling eligibility criteria for each; primary/registration details need to be submitted only once, but the application form (except registration details) must be filled and the fee paid separately for each post code. Fee once paid will not be refunded on any count, and the application once finally submitted cannot be withdrawn or modified.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Online Application
content_sections.text: The following documents must be uploaded along with the online application form, wherever applicable: Recent passport-size colour photograph of the candidate Signature of the candidate Fee receipt of Rs 500 as application fee, wherever applicable SSC / 10th certificate reflecting Date of Birth SSC / 10th Marksheet reflecting marks/percentage obtained (with grade-to-percentage conversion formula copy, if applicable, issued by the concerned Board/Institute) ITI / Trade / Apprentice Training Certificate Category / Community Certificate / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen Certificate or undertaking, in the prescribed Government of India format issued by the specified authority, if applicable Experience Certificate(s), if any Certificate(s) related to higher qualification, if any Aadhaar Card NOC from present employer (as per the prescribed Annexure format), if applicable Applications with unclear/smudged photographs, signatures, or essential documents will be rejected. Candidates should also ensure the photo and signature are uploaded at the correct places and are of the required size with the face clearly visible; failure to do so will result in rejection.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates must apply ONLINE only through the official website www.cmeri.res.in ; no other mode of application will be considered. The online application window is open from 4 July 2026, 10:00 AM, to 3 August 2026, 5:00 PM. Create/Verify Email ID – Candidates without a valid email ID should create one before applying. The registered email address and mobile number should remain active till the completion of the recruitment process, as communication will be made by CSIR-CMERI. Register on the Portal – Register with name and email address; after successful registration, log in using the credentials provided to access the Online Application Portal. Fill Application & Upload Documents – Complete the application form and upload all relevant supporting documents, ensuring the application is accurate and complete before finalizing. Pay Application Fee (wherever applicable) – Pay the fee online via SBI Collect, download the e-receipt, and upload it in the application along with the payment details at the appropriate place. Submit the Application – Click the ‘Submit’ button to finally submit the form. No further modifications or amendments will be permitted after submission. Keep a printout of the completed application form; candidates should NOT send a hard copy of the application after submission. Candidates from Universities/Institutes awarding CGPA/SGPA/OGPA/DGPA/CPI grades must convert these into percentage as per their University/Institute formula and upload a copy of the conversion formula issued by the Board/University/Institute. Print-outs of the application and payment details will not be available after 5:00 PM on 3 August 2026.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of online applications is 03 August 2026 at 05:00 PM IST, with the application window having opened on 04 July 2026 at 10:00 AM IST.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in CSIR CMERI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 27 Technician-1 (Group-II) vacancies across seven trades: Fitter, Electrician/Mechanic, Electronics Mechanic, Machinist, Foundryman, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle/Diesel), and Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for CSIR CMERI Technician posts?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 28 years, with relaxation available for SC, OBC-NCL, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need SSC/10th Standard with Science subjects and minimum 55% marks along with an ITI certificate or trade certificate, or alternatively 2 years of apprentice experience or 3 years of relevant work experience in a government organization.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for CSIR CMERI Technician posts?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed in Pay Level-2 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix (₹19,900–₹63,200), with total emoluments of approximately ₹39,000 per month.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for CSIR CMERI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves online application scrutiny followed by further selection stages as detailed in the official advertisement available on the CMERI website.

faq.question: Q7. Which trade has the highest vacancy in CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Fitter has the highest vacancy with 14 posts, followed by Electrician/Mechanic (Electrical Power Drives) and Electronics Mechanic/Instrument Mechanic with 4 posts each.

faq.question: Q8. Are any posts reserved for Ex-Servicemen in CSIR CMERI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, out of the total 27 posts, 2 posts are reserved horizontally for Ex-Servicemen (ESM).

faq.question: Q9. Where is CSIR-CMERI located and where will selected candidates work?
faq.answer: CSIR-CMERI is located at Mahatma Gandhi Avenue, Durgapur-713209 (West Bengal), and selected candidates will be posted at Durgapur or its extension centre at Ludhiana, Punjab.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates check the official CSIR CMERI notification and apply online?
faq.answer: The official notification and online application link are available on the CMERI website at www.cmeri.res.in under Career → Vacancies → Advt. No. 01/2026.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of online applications is 03 August 2026 at 05:00 PM IST, with the application window having opened on 04 July 2026 at 10:00 AM IST.

title: CSIR CMERI Technician Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 27 Fitter, Electrician and More Posts
